name: pepper
description: Runtime control for iOS simulator apps. Dylib injected at launch — no source modifications needed. 47 MCP tools for navigation, debugging, state inspection, and automation.

tools.name: look
tools.description: Primary observation tool — returns full UI hierarchy map

tools.name: tap
tools.description: Tap an element by accessibility label or coordinates

tools.name: scroll
tools.description: Scroll in a direction within a container

tools.name: scroll_to
tools.description: Scroll until a target element is visible

tools.name: swipe
tools.description: Swipe gesture with direction and distance

tools.name: input_text
tools.description: Type text into the focused field

tools.name: navigate
tools.description: Navigate to a named route or deep link

tools.name: back
tools.description: Go back in the navigation stack

tools.name: dismiss
tools.description: Dismiss a presented modal or sheet

tools.name: dialog
tools.description: Interact with system alerts and dialogs

tools.name: heap
tools.description: Inspect the ObjC/Swift heap for object instances

tools.name: layers
tools.description: Dump the CALayer tree for rendering inspection

tools.name: crash_log
tools.description: Retrieve crash logs and stack traces

tools.name: constraints
tools.description: Dump AutoLayout constraints with ambiguity detection

tools.name: find
tools.description: Search the view hierarchy for elements matching a query

tools.name: read_element
tools.description: Read detailed properties of a specific element

tools.name: highlight
tools.description: Highlight an element on screen for visual debugging

tools.name: hook
tools.description: Hook an ObjC method to log calls or modify behavior

tools.name: wait_for
tools.description: Wait for an element to appear

tools.name: wait_idle
tools.description: Wait for the app to become idle

tools.name: build_device
tools.description: Build the app for a physical device

tools.name: deploy
tools.description: Deploy and launch the app on simulator

tools.name: iterate
tools.description: Build, deploy, and verify in one step
